cards.title: Lovable Seeks $13.2B Valuation in New Funding
cards.body: The AI startup is reportedly in talks to double its valuation through a $300 million round led by Menlo Ventures. The funding would bring Lovable to a $13.2 billion valuation.

cards.title: Google's Deepfake Detector Debunks McConnell Hoax
cards.body: Google's AI system identified a fabricated image showing Senator Mitch McConnell in distress as an AI-generated deepfake. The detection demonstrates growing capability to combat election-related misinformation.

cards.title: SpaceX Releases Grok 4.5 AI Model
cards.body: Elon Musk's company launched Grok 4.5, an 'Opus-class model' positioning itself as cheaper and more efficient. The release offers a competitive alternative to leading AI models from other labs.

cards.title: General Intuition Bets Gaming Data Powers Robotics AI
cards.body: The startup is leveraging millions of hours of video game data to train foundation models for physical AI systems. The approach aims to enable smarter robots with minimal real-world training data.

cards.title: Google Photos Introduces AI Video Remix Tool
cards.body: The new feature applies cinematic relighting, background replacement, and artistic styles to video clips. Google Photos users can now enhance video content directly within the app.

cards.title: Video Games Over Internet for AGI Training
cards.body: General Intuition's CEO argues that large language models lack spatial-temporal understanding needed for AGI. Gaming data provides better training material than text-based internet data for physical reasoning.

cards.title: Meta Adds Recording Safeguard to AI Glasses
cards.body: The company implemented new protections to prevent secret recording with its AI glasses. The move contrasts with Meta's expanding data collection practices across AI products.

cards.title: OpenAI Launches New Voice Models for Live Conversations
cards.body: The updated voice mode enables simultaneous speaking and listening capabilities. The technology supports live translation and more natural real-time interactions.

cards.title: Prime Intellect Raises $130M for Enterprise AI Agents
cards.body: The 2024-founded startup secured Series A funding to help organizations build proprietary AI agent systems. Prime Intellect aims to reduce enterprise dependence on frontier AI labs.

cards.title: Fast-Growing AI Startups Accelerating Revenue Growth
cards.body: Multiple AI startups are demonstrating accelerating growth rates in revenue generation. The trend highlights increasing momentum in the AI startup ecosystem.

cards.title: Bezos-Backed Startup Leverages Gaming Data for AGI
cards.body: General Intuition uses gaming data to address LLM limitations in spatial-temporal reasoning. The approach differs from traditional large language model training methodologies.

cards.title: Former OpenAI Executive Joins Stoke Space Board
cards.body: Kevin Weil, previously at OpenAI, took a board position at rocket reuse company Stoke Space. The move signals growing tech industry interest in reusable rocket technology.

cards.title: French Startup ZML Releases Free AI Inference Software
cards.body: ZML, endorsed by Turing Award winner Yann LeCun, released ZML/LLMD to reduce AI inference costs. The software optimizes performance across multiple AI chip architectures.

cards.title: SambaNova Raises $1B at $11B Valuation
cards.body: The AI chip maker completed another mega-round just five months after previous funding. The valuation jump comes amid speculation of potential acquisition interest.

cards.title: Meta Launches Muse Image Generator
cards.body: The new image generation model supports advertising, decoration, and creator opportunities. Users are raising concerns about the use of their photos in training data.

cards.title: Open Source AI Success Not Hurting Anthropic
cards.body: Open source models and frontier labs appear to operate in complementary lifecycle phases. Competition between open and closed approaches hasn't eroded Anthropic's market position.

cards.title: Microsoft Cuts AI Spending With Proprietary Models
cards.body: The tech giant is reducing reliance on external AI services by scaling its own models. Microsoft joins other Silicon Valley companies in cost-cutting AI initiatives.

cards.title: Discord's AI Moderation Bug Wrongfully Banned Users
cards.body: The company confirmed that a moderation error affected accounts since May, with 200 additional users banned over the weekend. Discord identified and resolved the issue affecting harmless image detection.

cards.title: Claude Cowork Expands to Mobile and Web
cards.body: Users can now start AI-assisted tasks on desktop and monitor progress across mobile and web platforms. The cross-device functionality enables continued workflow even when laptops are closed.

cards.title: Savi Launches App to Detect AI Voice Scams
cards.body: The startup raised $7 million in seed funding to protect consumers from realistic AI-generated kidnapping hoaxes. The app launches Tuesday on iPhone and Android platforms.

cards.title: Lovable in talks to double valuation to $13.2B
cards.body: The AI startup is reportedly raising a $300 million round led by Menlo Ventures. This would nearly double Lovable's valuation in a competitive AI funding landscape.

cards.title: Google's deepfake detector debunks McConnell hoax
cards.body: Google's AI system identified an AI-generated image falsely depicting Senator Mitch McConnell in distress. The technology successfully prevented misinformation from spreading.

cards.title: SpaceX releases Grok 4.5 as Opus-class model
cards.body: Elon Musk's company unveiled the latest version of Grok, positioning it as a cheaper, more efficient alternative to competing AI models. The release continues the arms race among frontier AI labs.

cards.title: General Intuition bets on video game data for robotics AI
cards.body: The startup is using millions of hours of video game footage to train foundation models for physical AI. This approach could enable smarter robots with minimal real-world training data.

cards.title: Google Photos launches AI video remixing tool
cards.body: The new feature uses AI to apply cinematic effects, swap backgrounds, and add artistic styles to videos. Users can brighten dark footage or enhance clips with new visual treatments.

cards.title: Video games hold key to achieving AGI
cards.body: Industry leaders argue that large language models like ChatGPT lack spatial reasoning skills needed for artificial general intelligence. Video game data offers superior training for understanding physical movement and time.

cards.title: Meta adds safeguards to AI glasses while expanding data collection
cards.body: Meta introduced new protections to prevent secret recording with its AI glasses. The update contrasts with the company's broader strategy of expanding personal data usage for AI products.

cards.title: OpenAI releases voice models for live conversations
cards.body: The new voice mode can speak and listen simultaneously, enabling real-time translation capabilities. This represents a significant advancement in conversational AI technology.

cards.title: Prime Intellect raises $130M to democratize AI agent building
cards.body: The Series A funding will help enterprises build their own agentic systems without relying on frontier AI labs. Prime Intellect aims to reduce dependence on OpenAI and similar companies.

cards.title: AI startups accelerate revenue growth rates
cards.body: A cohort of fast-growing AI companies is expanding revenue at increasingly rapid rates. The trend reflects strong enterprise demand for AI solutions.

cards.title: ZML releases free inference software for AI cost reduction
cards.body: The French AI startup endorsed by Turing Award winner Yann LeCun released ZML/LLMD to optimize AI inference across multiple chip types. The tool could significantly lower operational costs for AI deployment.

cards.title: SambaNova raises $1B at $11B valuation
cards.body: The AI chip maker secured mega-round funding just 5 months after its previous investment. The valuation increase comes amid intense competition in the AI infrastructure space.

cards.title: Meta launches Muse Image generator amid privacy concerns
cards.body: The new AI image generator has multiple use cases including advertising and creative applications. Users are already pushing back over Meta's use of their photos in training.

cards.title: Open source AI doesn't hurt Anthropic yet
cards.body: Open source models and frontier labs like Anthropic are capturing different phases of the AI product lifecycle. Competition hasn't yet cannibalized revenue for premium AI providers.

cards.title: Microsoft cuts AI spending by relying on own models
cards.body: The tech giant is reducing its dependence on external AI providers and building more in-house capabilities. The shift reflects broader industry trends toward cost optimization in AI spending.

cards.title: Discord's AI moderation bug wrongfully bans users
cards.body: A flawed AI moderation system banned users over harmless images since May 2026. Discord identified and fixed the issue after 200 additional users were incorrectly banned.

cards.title: Claude Cowork expands to mobile and web platforms
cards.body: Users can now start AI tasks on desktop and track progress across devices seamlessly. The expansion makes Anthropic's AI assistant more accessible in multi-device workflows.

cards.title: Savi app protects consumers from AI-powered scams
cards.body: The startup raised $7 million in seed funding to combat realistic AI scams including deepfake ransom calls. The app launches on iPhone and Android to detect AI-generated threats.

cards.title: Sam Altman's OpenAI wealth-sharing plan resurfaces
cards.body: Altman's proposal for Americans to share AI-generated wealth is back in the spotlight. The Financial Times recently covered the ongoing discussion about AI's economic benefits.

cards.title: IT leaders navigate AI architecture scaling challenges
cards.body: Rapid AI capability advances and the shift toward agentic systems require new organizational investments. IT leaders face uncertainty about which AI infrastructure decisions will remain valuable.

cards.title: Ivy League professor uses in-person final to catch AI cheating
cards.body: A suspicious professor required students to complete exams on campus instead of remotely. Test scores dropped 50% in-person, suggesting widespread AI usage in remote assignments.

cards.title: Man used Grok to generate child sexual abuse material
cards.body: A lawsuit alleges the defendant created 7,000 illegal images using SpaceX's AI model Grok. The case highlights growing misuse of generative AI tools for illegal purposes.

cards.title: Google updates Android Bench with new LLMs
cards.body: Google's benchmarking tool now tests more large language models. Notably, Google's Gemini continues to underperform compared to competing models in various metrics.

cards.title: Hackers use popular AI tools to assemble botnets
cards.body: Security researchers found that 9 of the most widely-used AI platforms can be exploited to create massive botnet networks. The vulnerability reveals significant security gaps in frontier AI systems.
